abstract: This paper examines the nuance of linguistic "spin" in the addresses delivered by Barack Obama and Hassan Rouhani at the United Nations in September 2013. Drawing upon Halliday’s Systemic Functional Linguistics, the study utilizes transitivity and modality analyses to illustrate how both leaders leveraged language to weave ideology and power into their rhetoric. By deconstructing these linguistic choices, the paper demonstrates how the presidents articulated their authority, national capabilities, and strategic policies. Ultimately, this research seeks to clarify the underlying political implications of these speeches, making the subtle maneuvers of diplomatic discourse more accessible to a general audience.
